items.title: Trump says Iran 'collapsing financially' over Hormuz closure
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said Tuesday that Iran was "collapsing financially" over the closure of the vital Strait of Hormuz. "Iran is collapsing financially! Trump posted earlier that Teheran said it supported the closure of the strait to "save face" under the US blockade of Iranian ports.

items.title: RM5b MSME aid only ‘small win,’ more needed to tackle crisis, says Guan Eng
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — DAP advisor Lim Guan Eng has described the government’s latest RM5 billion aid package for small businesses as a “small win,” urging Putrajaya to implement bolder measures like making the first RM50,000 of all loans interest-free and collateral-free. The Bagan MP was responding to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s announcement of several measures to support micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs), including the new loan facility and a one-year delay for e-invoicing implementation. “I urge the prime minister to approve the first RM 50,000 portion of the entire loan be deemed interest-free and collateral free.

items.title: HSA clarifies woman in viral ward deposit incident not a staff member
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Sultanah Aminah Hospital (HSA) management has clarified that a woman involved in a viral incident, where she allegedly posed as a staff member to collect ward deposits, is not an employee. HSA, in a post on Threads, said it took a serious view of the allegation involving an individual requesting cash payments for ward deposits without issuing official receipts. Earlier, a woman claimed her mother was asked to pay RM30 in cash to the individual when registering her father's admission to a ward.

items.title: Capital market reforms on track under five-year masterplan amid uncertainties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's capital market is on track to deliver reforms, strengthen competitiveness and enhance investor protection under the Capital Market Masterplan 2026–2030. Malaysia's capital market expanded 3.2 per cent to a record RM4.3 trillion in 2025, from RM4.2 trillion a year earlier, supported by stronger corporate bond issuances and higher fund management inflows. The Islamic capital market remained robust, growing 4.31 per cent to RM2.7 trillion.

items.title: 'Donations gone' before reaching fire victims as 'outsiders' strip moving lorry
items.summary: SANDAKAN: A lorry driver whose vehicle carrying donations for fire victims in Kampung Bahagia was stormed in a viral video said he was left shocked and saddened by the incident. Jeffry Ayung, 36, said the incident occurred on the first day of the fire when he was delivering aid contributed by the Sungai Manila Neighbourhood Watch (KRT) and Sungai Manila mosque. Contacted today, Jeffry said the situation turned unexpectedly tense as he was heading to the designated collection point when his lorry was approached by unidentified individuals.

items.title: AmBank backs Gajahsuria Power's solar project with RM102mil loan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AmBank Group has extended RM102.40 million loan to Gajahsuria Power Sdn Bhd to support the development of a 29.99 megawatts alternating current (MWac) large scale solar 5 (LSS5) project in Kampung Gajah, Perak. Established in January 2025, the company is jointly owned by Sun Energy Ventures Sdn Bhd, Constant Energy Malaysia Holding Sdn Bhd (CEMH) and BayWa r.e Energy Solutions Sdn Bhd. Constant Energy chief executive officer Frank Constant said the project showcases the company's commitment to supporting Malaysia's energy transition while expanding its renewable energy footprint across Southeast Asia.

items.title: Viral photo of children bathing in Kuantan fountain spark safety, hygiene concerns
items.summary: KUANTAN: A photo of a group of children bathing in a fountain at Kuantan City Garden opposite Kompleks Teruntum has gone viral, sparking debate online. Social media users also highlighted concerns over the lack of parental supervision, while others questioned the Kuantan City Council's (MBK) monitoring of public amenities, particularly the fountain in the town centre. Hani Syazani said MBK will also deploy enforcement officers to conduct regular checks at Kuantan City Garden, especially during weekends.

items.title: Ikram suspends three members caught in RM230m ‘zakat’ fund scandal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Pertubuhan Ikram Malaysia (Ikram) has suspended three members arrested by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) in connection with a massive investigation into the alleged misappropriation of approximately RM230 million in zakat funds. The move comes after two individuals, including the deputy chairman of a non-governmental organisation (NGO), were remanded for four days starting today to assist in the MACC probe. In a statement today, Ikram president Badlishah Sham Baharin said that the organisation views the matter seriously and is extending its full cooperation to the MACC.

items.title: India orders school water bells to beat heat
items.summary: NEW DELHI: Schools in India's capital must ring regular bells to remind schoolchildren to drink water as the megacity gears up to face heatwave conditions, a new city order has said. A heatwave in May 2024 in New Delhi saw temperatures match the capital's previous record high: 49.2C clocked in 2022. That year was India's hottest year since thorough records began in 1901, with sizzling temperatures following a global pattern of extreme weather driven by climate change.

items.title: Senior citizen jailed four months for offensive post on royal institution
items.summary: SEPANG: A 64-year-old man was sentenced to four months' jail by the Sessions Court today after pleading guilty to making offensive posts involving the royal institution two years ago. Mat Derus had posted the comment using the Facebook account Mat Derus at 2.15am on Aug 20, 2024. In a separate case, 52-year-old army veteran Marzuki Ismail pleaded not guilty to making offensive posts involving race, the judiciary, and the royal institution (3R) last December, using the Facebook account Marzuki Ismail.

items.title: UK agrees ban on cigarette sales for people born after 2008
items.summary: The Tobacco and Vapes Bill aims to stop anyone born after Jan 1, 2009 (now aged 17) from taking up smoking. It will also give the government new powers to restrict the flavours and packaging of vapes and ban vaping in places where smoking is already banned. Hazel Cheeseman, director of public health charity Action on Smoking and Health (ASH), told LBC radio station that the bill was a "decisive turning point for public health."

items.title: Australia's online watchdog targets Roblox and Minecraft over extreme content
items.summary: SYDNEY: Australia's internet watchdog raised fears on Wednesday that popular online gaming platforms such as Roblox and Minecraft were being used by "predatory adults" to target children. eSafety boss Julie Inman Grant said research had shown the vast majority of Australian children played some sort of online game. Online gaming platforms Roblox, Minecraft, Fortnite and Steam will be required to show how they are identifying and eliminating online harms, Inman Grant said.

items.title: US military says key weapons system staying in South Korea
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States has not moved a key missile defence system out of South Korea, a US military official said Tuesday, following reports that Washington was shifting parts of it to the Middle East. The report had caused disquiet in South Korea, where the system is a pillar of national defence against nuclear-armed North Korea. The commander of US forces in South Korea said Washington had "not moved any THAAD systems" out of the country.

items.title: Shop told to explain RM30 charge for repairing water-damaged calculator
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Kelantan Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry has issued a notice to a shop owner to explain a RM30 charge for repairing a water-damaged calculator. The ministry's state deputy director Aswadi Jaafar said the notice was issued yesterday under the Price Control and Anti-Profiteering Act. Aswadi said the ministry acted following a public complaint.

items.title: Mara offers 'Peace of Mind' counselling for struggling borrowers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) has called on its borrowers facing financial difficulties to attend a special counselling session, Peace of Mind, organised as part of a five-day carnival in conjunction with its 60th anniversary celebrations. Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the initiative was introduced with the cost-of-living crisis in mind, with the aim of easing financial burdens. There will be a special session, Peace of Mind, throughout the five-day carnival, which will run from 9am to 10pm daily," Asyraf told reporters after launching the 2026 Mara Chairman's Cup Public Speaking Competition at the Medan Mara building today.

items.title: Gobind demands immediate, transparent update from A-G on Durian Tunggal shooting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DAP national chairman Gobind Singh Deo today called on the Attorney-General to provide an immediate and transparent update on the Durian Tunggal shooting case. He said that about five months have passed since the incident occurred, with no definitive action taken against any individuals involved. "Despite the passage of time, there appears to be a lack of definitive action taken against any individuals involved.

items.title: Saifuddin questions Muhyiddin's focus amid Middle East crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Indera Mahkota Member of Parliament Datuk Seri Saifuddin Abdullah has questioned Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin's priorities, raising concerns over efforts to replace the opposition leader while the country grapples with the impact of the Middle East crisis. In a video shared on X, Saifuddin said the opposition should be focusing on the broader implications of the conflict, particularly its potential impact on Malaysia's economy and security. Saifuddin said the ongoing crisis posed direct risks to economic stability and national security.

items.title: North Korean hackers suspected of US$300mil crypto heist
items.summary: SEOUL: A notorious North Korean hacking group is likely behind the theft of nearly US$300 mn in cryptocurrency over the weekend, an affected party has said, in the biggest known crypto heist this year. During the hack, two blockchain servers hosted by another crypto tech application called LayerZero were compromised, KelpDAO said Tuesday. "Preliminary indicators suggest attribution to a highly sophisticated state actor, likely DPRK's Lazarus Group," LayerZero said, using the initials of North Korea's official name.

items.title: RM230m zakat funds probe: Two suspects, including NGO deputy chairman, remanded for four days over alleged misappropriation
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 22 — Two individuals, including the deputy chairman of a non-governmental organisation (NGO), have been remanded for four days, from today, to assist in investigations into the alleged misappropriation of about RM230 million in zakat funds. Earlier, the two individuals arrived at the Shah Alam Court complex at 9.07am in a MACC vehicle. Yesterday, the Selangor MACC arrested three individuals, including a deputy chairman of an NGO, on suspicion of misappropriating about RM230 million in zakat funds.

items.title: Mara marks 60th year with national public speaking push
items.summary: A total of 102 entries were received, with 40 finalists – 25 from Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM) and 15 from Mara colleges – selected for the final. Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the initiative reflects the agency's commitment to developing human capital beyond academic achievement. The competition, themed "Mara at 60, Charting Success, Building Civilisation", offers RM10,000 in prizes.

items.title: Khaled Nordin: Forest City desalination plant moved to Lok Heng as 4,000 hit by water disruption
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: More than 4,000 residents in Felda Lok Heng and Felda Waha here have been affected by prolonged water supply disruptions following a sharp drop in river levels caused by extended dry weather. Kota Tinggi member of parliament Datuk Seri Khaled Nordin said water levels at Sungai Ulu Sedili Kecil had fallen to a critical level, rendering it unable to support operations at the Lok Heng water treatment plant. Khaled, who is also the defence minister, said the Johor state government, Ranhill SAJ and relevant agencies had explored alternative sources, including groundwater extraction via tube wells and water transfer from Sungai Lukah.

items.title: Exco: Selangor considering seawater desalination for water security
items.summary: He said the construction of the infrastructure could increase clean water production capacity by about 300 million litres per day (MLD) for use in the state. Izham said Selangor’s current water reserve margin stands at around 17.4 per cent, or 1,130 MLD, while total clean water production capacity from all 34 water treatment plants (LRAs) is 6,500 MLD, with average actual demand at 5,370 MLD. He said Pengurusan Air Selangor Sdn Bhd (Air Selangor) also expects the required annual water reserve capacity to increase by about 20 per cent over the next five years.

items.title: Johor man charged with bribing police RM18,500 to ignore friends’ criminal histories
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 22 — A 31-year-old factory supervisor pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to a charge of bribing a police officer with RM18,500 to allegedly overlook his and his friends' criminal records. The accused, M. Dhivan, made the plea after the charge was read to him in Bahasa Melayu before Judge Datuk Che Wan Zaidi Che Wan Ibrahim. According to the charge sheet, Dhivan is accused of corruptly giving RM18,500 in cash to Sergeant Koshaziza Idris of the Segamat district police headquarters' patrol unit.

items.title: Don't treat illness, disease prevention as seasonal, says deputy health minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Private healthcare providers are essential in the Health Ministry's shift towards upstream healthcare. Deputy Health Minister Datuk Hanifah Hajar Taib said prevention has long been central to the ministry's mission, driven through healthy lifestyle campaigns, community-based programmes, as well as screening and awareness efforts on non-communicable diseases, mental health, infectious disease control and injury prevention. "We are moving healthcare upstream, shifting from reactive treatment to prevention.

items.title: Back home, Hong Kong fire victim gathers memories from the ashes
items.summary: HONG KONG: Dorz Cheung was at work when one of Hong Kong's deadliest fires broke out in November. Cheung, 34, said as he left the apartment, carrying some of the things most dear to his heart: his running medals, family photos, his grandmother's 50-year-old journals documenting her reflections on the Bible. FOUND LATE GRANDFATHER'S BELONGINGS In Cheung's room on Tuesday, a giant plush teddy bear rested on ruffled-up bedsheets.

items.title: WFH a short-term buffer, not structural fix — economist
items.summary: The Centre for Market Education chief executive officer, Carmelo Ferlito, said the government's push towards remote working is likely aimed at "buying time" amid ongoing global uncertainty, rather than addressing structural issues. While fewer commuters on the road could lead to marginal fuel savings, Ferlito said such reductions would not be enough to offset the rising cost of subsidies driven by global oil price volatility. Flexibility and clear communication from the government are key," he said.

items.title: Mother of hit-and-run victim appeals for driver to come forward
items.summary: KUCHING: A 68-year-old mother is appealing for the driver involved in a March hit-and-run incident that cost her only son his left leg to come forward and assist police investigations. Afidah Suhaili said her family harbours no resentment and only hopes the individual will take responsibility for the accident. Putra Aqmal said he tried to avoid the vehicle, but his left leg struck the side of the car, throwing him onto the road.

items.title: RM230mil zakat misappropriation: Two men remanded for four days [WATCH]
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Two men, including the deputy chairman of a non-governmental organisation, have been remanded for four days to assist in investigations into the alleged misappropriation of RM230 million in zakat funds. Bernama reported that the duo, who were arrested in the Klang Valley yesterday, arrived at the court complex at 9.07am in an MACC van. A third person in his 50s was also arrested yesterday at the Selangor MACC office.

items.title: Federal Court dismisses challenge against whipping as cruel and inhumane
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 22 — The Federal Court today upheld the constitutionality of whipping, ruling in a split decision that the punitive measure does not violate the fundamental liberties guaranteed by the Federal Constitution. Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh, delivering the majority judgment, dismissed the argument that the sentence was cruel, inhumane, or discriminatory. However, the nation’s top court distinguished whipping from a death sentence, stating that its purpose is punitive, not to deprive a person of their right to life.

items.title: Cost hikes may force 30 pct of padi farmers to skip planting season
items.summary: ALOR STAR: About 30 per cent of padi farmers nationwide, including those in Sabah and Sarawak, are expected to skip the new planting season due to rising operational costs. Malaysian Padi Farmers Association (Pesawah) chairman Abdul Rashid Yob said of the estimated 200,000 farmers across the country, many were struggling with high costs, particularly following diesel price hikes. "Ploughing costs have increased by over 50 per cent, squeezing the income of already struggling farmers.

items.title: Mother pleads for hit-and-run Avanza driver to come forward after son loses leg in Kuching
items.summary: KUCHING, April 22 — A mother is appealing for the driver involved in a hit-and-run incident in late March that left her only son with his left leg amputated to come forward and lodge a police report to assist with investigations. Afidah Suhaili, 68, said her family harbours no resentment and only hopes the individual will take responsibility for the incident. She also urged anyone with CCTV or dashcam footage of the incident to assist with police investigations.

items.title: Social Welfare Dept denies role in child placement, says arrangement was private
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Social Welfare Department has refuted viral allegations that they had requested an individual to take over the care of a child, clarifying that the arrangement was a private agreement between the child's mother and the individual. "The care of the child was a private arrangement between the biological mother and the individual, conducted without the Social Welfare Department's involvement," the statement read. Following the referral, a Social Welfare Department protector conducted an investigation and assessment in line with the "best interests of the child" principle under the Child Act 2001 (Act 611).

items.title: Malaysia seen as safe-haven energy investment hub – MBSB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is emerging as a key investment destination in Southeast Asia (SEA), as capital shifts towards regions capable of delivering immediate or near-term energy production to offset potential supply disruptions, MBSB Investment Bank Bhd said. MBSB said the offering is structured to appeal to a broad spectrum of investors, combining lower-risk mature assets with higher-impact frontier exploration opportunities. It said the offering includes nine exploration blocks across Malaysia, spanning high-impact frontier areas such as the Sandakan Basin, high-potential emerging areas such as the West Sarawak Basin, and near-field mature areas such as the Malay Basin.

items.title: Guarding sovereignty in the Strait of Malacca
items.summary: THE Strait of Malacca is a critical international shipping lane heavily traversed by merchant vessels to and from numerous nations to locations worldwide. It is therefore imperative that Indonesia, Malaysia, and Singapore cooperate more effectively in preserving the sovereignty of nations that transit through the waterway, ensuring global marine transportation security, and upholding the law of the sea, particularly those laws applicable to straits used for international navigation. Indonesia, Malaysia and Singapore are the stakeholders and actors responsible for ensuring safety at sea in the busy Strait.

items.title: Business administration graduate finds greener pastures in dairy farming
items.summary: The Slim River native chose instead to become his own boss by venturing into dairy farming. Now 52, Christol runs a five-hectare farm with 70 Friesian Sahiwal dairy cows imported from Australia and New Zealand, alongside 120 goats. "Success in dairy farming is not easy.

items.title: Burst pipe triggers water disruption in JB, Kota Tinggi and Pontian
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A burst pipe at the Sungai Johor water treatment plant disrupted supply to vast areas across the state capital, Kota Tinggi and Pontian today, affecting residential, industrial and key public facilities. In a statement, Ranhill SAJ said the incident forced the postponement of scheduled maintenance at the plant, resulting in an unplanned disruption across multiple zones. Among the worst-hit areas are Tiram, Skudai, Iskandar Puteri and parts of Kota Tinggi, involving entire townships, industrial hubs and high-density residential zones.

items.title: YTL Cement issues offer document for Concrete Engineering takeover offer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: YTL Cement Bhd today issued the offer document in relation to its unconditional mandatory takeover offer for the remaining shares it does not own in Concrete Engineering Products Bhd, giving the first closing date of the offer as May 13 at 5pm. YTL Cement said the acquisition and subsequent offer are in line with its strategy to expand its precast concrete manufacturing segment and strengthen vertical integration across its cement, aggregates and construction materials businesses. Concrete Engineering shares rose three sen to RM2.63 at 10.56am, with 42,000 shares traded.

items.title: Selangor accelerates greening campaign after planting eight million trees since 2022
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 22 — The Selangor government is on track to achieve its goal of planting 11 million trees, having already surpassed eight million since its state-wide greening campaign began, the state assembly was told today. State Public Health and Environment Committee chairman Jamaliah Jamaluddin confirmed that the initiative, launched in May 2022, has recorded a total of 8,050,011 trees planted across the state. New target set for 2026 To maintain momentum, Jamaliah announced a new target for 2026, calling on each local authority and government agency to plant approximately 100,000 trees throughout the year.

items.title: Actor Ako Mustapha alleges son bullied by five students; police confirm report
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Police have confirmed receiving a report regarding the alleged bullying of actor Ako Mustapha’s 10-year-old son, Mikael Iskandar, by five other students at a school here. Wangsa Maju District Police Chief Assistant Commissioner Annas Sulaiman verified that his agency has been notified, Berita Harian reported. “I confirm that the Wangsa Maju District Police Headquarters (IPD) has received a police report as stated.

items.title: Sandakan fire witness says sorry after video of firefighter abuse goes viral
items.summary: SANDAKAN, April 22 — A man has apologised to the Fire and Rescue Department for using abusive language in a viral video recorded during the recent fire at Kampung Bahagia here. Rozaizar Rozainie, 39, said his actions were driven by heightened emotions and stress as he witnessed the chaotic scene, including children panicking as the flames spread. The Fire and Rescue Department had earlier said firefighting operations faced several challenges, including narrow access routes, the absence of fire hydrants, and strong winds that accelerated the spread of the fire.

items.title: Ahmad Zahid: Umno’s 80 years more than just a number, it’s a legacy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has described the party as an enduring platform of struggle, ahead of its 80th anniversary on May 11. The Deputy Prime Minister said that since its inception, Umno has embarked on a long journey that has shaped the country’s direction, from independence to development, through sacrifice, leadership and relentless effort. Ahmad Zahid said Umno, founded in 1946 as a movement to oppose the Malayan Union and defend the country’s sovereignty, was born out of the spirit of unity and courage of the people.

items.title: Sipitang residents warned after crocodile seen near water treatment plant
items.summary: He said the crocodile was seen swimming in the middle of the river. The sighting raised concerns among residents, particularly those who frequent the river for daily activities. Recent reports also highlighted crocodile sightings in coastal waters in Labuan, raising broader safety concerns among communities.

items.title: Survey and Mapping D-G charged with abusing position to fund travel abroad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — The Director-General of the Survey and Mapping Department, who also serves as the chairman of the Land Surveyors Board, was charged at the Sessions Court here today with abusing his position to secure funds totalling over RM136,000 for his own attendance at international forums. Datuk Hazri Hassan, 57, pleaded not guilty to two charges read before Judge Suzana Hussin, Free Malaysia Today reported. He is accused of using his dual roles to deliberate on and approve funding from the Land Surveyors Board for his participation in forums held in Saudi Arabia and the United States.

items.title: MACC nabs second suspect in Sabah police station cleaning contract fraud
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 22 — A civil servant in Sabah has been detained by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) for allegedly colluding in a scheme to submit false claims for a cleaning contract at a state police station worth approximately RM500,000. According to sources, the suspect was detained at approximately 3.30pm yesterday after arriving at the Sabah MACC office to provide a statement. Sabah MACC director Datuk Mohd Fuad Bee Basrah confirmed the detention when contacted.

items.title: Rain, rain, go away: Penang’s dry weather spells ‘creamy’ dream for durian lovers
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 22 — The King of Fruits has made an early and impressive entrance in Penang, with durian lovers already finding early-season favourites like D604, Lipan, and even Musang King on sale weeks ahead of schedule. He prices the D604 at RM25 per kg, while Lipan ranges from RM20 to RM30 per kg depending on size. For Musang King, prices vary by grade, with top-tier fruit going for RM58 per kg and lower grades available for around RM38 per kg.

items.title: Sandakan fire: Eight relief centres set up for 1,912 residents who lost homes
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 22 — A total of 1,912 victims from 385 families affected by the Kampung Bahagia fire in Sandakan on Sunday (April 19) are currently housed at eight temporary evacuation centres (PPS) as of 8 am today. The Sabah Disaster Management Committee (JPBN) Secretariat said the victims are being accommodated at PPS Dewan Projek Perumahan Rakyat Batu Sapi, Dewan Sekolah Kebangsaan Gas, Dewan Sekolah Kebangsaan Kampung Bahagia, Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Batu Sapi, Dewan Sibuga, and Sekolah Kebangsaan Karamunting, all of which were opened on Sunday. “Two more PPS were opened on Monday, namely Dewan Serbaguna Warga Tua and Sekolah Menengah Jenis Kebangsaan Tiong Hwa.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh announces major cut in KL concert deposit rates from May 1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 222 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) will reduce its security deposit rate for concerts, stage and cultural performances to support the extension of Visit Malaysia 2026 to 2027. Minister in the Prime Minsiter’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the new rates will take effect on May 1, with deposits for stage or cultural performances involving local artistes reduced from RM10,000 to RM4,000, while the rate for concerts is set at RM10,000. “For foreign artistes, the deposit for stage and cultural performances is reduced fromRM30,000 to RM8,000, and concerts from RM30,000 to RM15,000,” she told reporters after attending the Arts, Live Festivals and Events Association (ALIFE) Malaysia here last night.

items.title: RM100 side hustle gone wrong: Kelantan police nab teen using brother’s Triton for people-smuggling
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 22 — Police arrested an 18-year-old youth in Kampung Bunohan on Monday for allegedly acting as a ‘tekong darat’ (ground coordinator) and being paid RM100 for each illegal immigrant transported. General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade Commander SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the suspect was arrested at about 4am following a follow-up operation after a vehicle was intercepted along the Terbok-Kelaboran Belukar route at 11.30 pm the previous night. “However, two men managed to escape, while five foreigners were found hiding in the back seat,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: Fahmi moots extra aid for event organisers amid Visit Malaysia Year extension
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — The Communications Ministry will propose to the Cabinet additional assistance for event organisers following the extension of the Visit Malaysia (VM) 2026 campaign to 2027, said its Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. Fahmi, who is also the MADANI Government spokesperson, said the ministry has also requested MyCreative Ventures to engage with all borrowers, particularly among artistes and event organisers, to restructure their loans following the extension of VM 2026. He said this to reporters after officiating a dinner of the Arts, Live Festival and Events Association (ALIFE) Malaysia here last night.

items.title: Police arrest Kelantan couple for alleged abuse of 10-year-old nephew
items.summary: KUALA KRAI, April 22 — A 10-year-old boy was allegedly abused by his aunt and uncle in Kampung Sungai Mas, Machang, after being separated from his four siblings. Kelantan Social Welfare Department (JKM) director Che Samsuzuki Che Noh confirmed the case. "JKM counsellors will carry out appropriate interventions to assist the child in overcoming his trauma.

items.title: Survey and Mapping D-G to face corruption charges today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — The Director-General of the Department of Survey and Mapping Malaysia (JUPEM) is expected to be charged in the Sessions Court here today in connection with an alleged misappropriation of funds. A search of the court’s filing system showed that the proceedings against the senior official are scheduled to take place before Judge Suzana Hussin at 9 am. On April 17, Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki confirmed that the Director-General and a senior officer from the same department would be produced in court this week.

items.title: Anwar in Sabah to assess massive Kampung Bahagia fire damage
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 22 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to visit Sabah today to assess the situation following a major fire in Kampung Bahagia, Sandakan, which destroyed 1,000 homes on Sunday (April 19). Anwar will meet victims at PPS Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Gas at 4.35 pm, followed by a stop at PPS Dewan Program Perumahan Rakyat (PPR) Taman Batu Sapi at 5 pm. His working visit will conclude with a stop at PPS Dewan Lintas Sibuga at 5.30 pm.

items.title: What Meta’s new Teen Accounts will look and feel like for Malaysian users under 16
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Meta Platforms recently rolled out enhanced safeguards for teenagers through its revamped “Teen Accounts” on Instagram, placing younger users under even stricter default settings for privacy, content exposure and parental controls. Unlike a standard Instagram account, where privacy and safety settings must be manually configured, Teen Accounts come pre-set with stricter safeguards. This also includes expanding existing protections for Teen Accounts registered on Instagram.

items.title: MACC detains three men for alleged misappropriation of RM230m in zakat funds, luxury vehicles, cash and properties confiscated
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 – The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) detained three individuals, including a deputy chairman of a non-governmental organisation (NGO), in connection with the alleged misappropriation of zakat funds. According to The Star , the arrests were made by the Selangor branch of the MACC today. Selangor MACC director Mohd Azwan Ramli confirmed the arrests, stating that the case is being investigated under the MACC Act 2009.

items.title: Show must go on: Melaka chief minister says no reason for state polls to be delayed
items.summary: MELAKA, April 21 — Melaka has no solid grounds to postpone the state election, which is expected to take place this year in accordance with the State Constitution, said Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh. He said that, under the State Constitution, the Barisan Nasional (BN)-led state government’s term ends in December. Kota Melaka MP Khoo Poay Tiong, who is also the state DAP chairman, meanwhile suggested that the Melaka government submit a petition to the King to declare a state of emergency to postpone the state elections, following the global supply crisis.

items.title: Government is focusing on policy adjustments to address cost pressures, global supply crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — The government is focusing on policy adjustments to manage cost pressures while simultaneously ensuring the supply of basic necessities needed to address the impact of the global supply crisis. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the situation in the Strait of Hormuz has not yet shown a convincing resolution, despite certain diplomatic developments. "The issue is not only whether the physical supply is disrupted or not, but also involves risk premiums, insurance costs, logistics, delivery delays, and disruptions to the global supply chain.

items.title: Economy minister: Global supply crisis is starting to affect local prices
items.summary: He said that monitoring of selected food prices for the period April 13 to 19, 2026 shows mixed price movements. "The average price of standard chicken increased by 2.8 per cent from RM9.09 per kilogramme to RM9.33 per kilogramme, while the price of beef declined by 5.0 per cent to RM35.65 per kilogramme, and Grade C eggs remained stable at an average of RM3.66 per 10 eggs. "For fish and vegetable categories, the price of mackerel decreased to RM16.43 per kilogramme from RM17.08, while the average price of mustard greens rose from RM5.89 to RM6.21 per kilogramme, and the price of green spinach remained stable at RM5.26 per kilogramme,” he said in a special briefing on the global supply crisis broadcast live on local television today.

items.title: PM Anwar: Govt will not remain idle in facing global crisis, the people’s interests remain paramount
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today emphasised that the government will not remain idle in facing the global supply crisis which is becoming increasingly complex. In a post on his official X account today, Anwar said every measure taken is purposeful based on the people’s interest and aimed at ensuring Malaysia remains prepared, resilient, and protected from energy supply shocks. Anwar today received a courtesy visit from National Gas Company SAOG Oman chairman Sh Abdulla Suleiman Hamed Al Harthy to boost strategic cooperation and explore new investment opportunities that can help ensure the stability of the country’s energy supply.

items.title: As global conflicts loom, Putrajaya Forum pushes Asean to aspire for information dominance and bolster climate security
items.summary: The Putrajaya Forum took place today against this backdrop, in conjunction with the 19th Defence Services Asia (DSA) and National Security (NATSEC) Asia 2026 exhibition, at the Malaysia International Trade and Exhibition Centre (MITEC). Setting the stage for the discourse, the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah stressed on the need for governance and regulation of emerging technologies before deploying them. Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah delivers his royal keynote address during the Putrajaya Forum 2026 at MITEC in Kuala Lumpur on April 21, 2026.

items.title: King offers prayers for Johor Haj pilgrims
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 21 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia has extended his prayers to 500 prospective haj pilgrims from the Johor state delegation departing for the Holy Land for the 1447H/2026M season. In a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar’s Facebook page, His Majesty expressed his hope that all pilgrims would be granted safety, good health and a mabrur haj. At the same time, His Majesty extended food pack contributions to the pilgrims through Yayasan Sultan Ibrahim Johor (YSIJ).
